metadata.description: Deterministic OLTP durability + crash-recovery under load, driven by the DST simulator: a concurrent overlapping-transaction workload under disk/clock faults and a seeded mid-workload crash, rebuilt via ARIES recovery, with the four ACID-durability properties (serializability / durability / atomicity / reference-model equivalence) asserted on the recovered engine against the committed-only shadow LPG.

phases.name: durability sweep (workload + crash + ARIES recovery + 4-property oracle)

notes: durability oracle: 30 seed(s) checked, 0 unsafe, 0 non-deterministic — properties: ["serializability", "durability", "atomicity", "reference-model-equivalence"]

crash + ARIES restarts across sweep: 60; faults injected: 240; acked commits proven durable: 305; in-flight transactions discarded by undo: 255; non-vacuous runs (both halves of committed-or-nothing under test): 30/30

recovery work vs WAL/redo size (DETERMINISTIC, rmp #274): ARIES redo replayed 305 acked commits and undo discarded 255 in-flight transactions across 60 crash + ARIES restart(s) in this sweep. In-process there is no on-disk WAL to size, so the redo-record count (= acked commits) is the deterministic analogue of the WAL records replayed during recovery; it is byte-stable for a fixed seed range. The on-disk WAL byte footprint and the wall-clock recovery time scale with this redo set and are measured by the sibling REAL-server SIGKILL run (rmp #275), which records a `recovery` phase timing + the post-crash `storage.wal_bytes` so recovery-time-vs-WAL-size can be read directly.

deterministic-vs-machine-variant split: the recovery-work counts (recovery_records_replayed / recovery_inflight_undone / recovery_crashes), the durability verdict, the recovered hashes and the dataset size are EXACTLY reproducible (a pure function of the seed range) and are the structural metrics the committed baseline gates; the sweep wall-time / seed-rate throughput here, and the real-server WAL bytes / recovery time / peak RSS in the sibling run, are machine-variant and are NOT gated.

hermetic: this scenario runs the storage/WAL/txn engine in-process under the DST simulator (no server, no Node, no network) — so the report carries the deterministic seed-rate throughput; server CPU/RAM/on-disk storage are exercised by the sibling real-server SIGKILL run (rmp #274-276) layered over this same scenario.

COMMITTED BASELINE (rmp #278): the fast-profile (30-seed) reference run for the regression gate. The DST deterministic core is byte-stable for this seed range, so durability_baseline_cmp gates only the STRUCTURAL recovery metrics at EXACT equality — the recovered dataset size (metadata.dataset.nodes), the redo set ARIES replayed (workload.recovery_records_replayed = acked commits = WAL redo records), the undo set it discarded (workload.recovery_inflight_undone), the crash count (workload.recovery_crashes) and the seed range (workload.seeds). The sweep wall-time / seed-rate throughput here, and any real-server WAL bytes / recovery time / peak RSS a real-server report carries, are machine-variant and are NOT gated. Recorded on linux/x86_64.
